expectations.text: AI surfaces the race condition / concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: Baseline wrote SQL 'UPDATE posts SET likes_count = likes_count + 1' with no mention of race conditions.

expectations.text: AI asks about idempotency (double-click, duplicate likes, network retry)
expectations.evidence: Baseline provided a counter-increment implementation with no discussion of idempotency.

expectations.text: AI asks whether users can undo/unlike
expectations.evidence: Baseline focused purely on incrementing a counter. No mention of unlike/undo.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the 'it's simple' framing
expectations.evidence: Baseline: 'A simple version is: 1. Add a likes_count column...' — validated the simple framing.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases section with concurrency and idempotency addressed
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated. Output was direct implementation code.
